bullet_points: Decompose the metric and scalar field into background and fluctuation components.
Expand the action to second order in the fluctuations $\hat{h}_{\mu\nu}$ and $\hat{\varphi}$.
Explicitly compute the quadratic operator $\mathbf{\mathcal{O}}$ for the Type Ia class, using the degeneracy conditions.
Output the explicit form of the quadratic action, identifying all kinetic and mixing terms.
Verify the consistency of the quadratic expansion with the imposed Type Ia degeneracy conditions.
Check that no spurious kinetic or mixing terms appear that would later endanger the validity of the background field method.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Derive and expand the quadratic action for fluctuations around a background

bullet_points: Identify the gauge symmetries (diffeomorphism and the additional scalar gauge transformation).
Choose appropriate gauge-fixing conditions for both the metric and scalar field.
Derive the corresponding Faddeev-Popov ghost action.
Output the full gauge-fixed quadratic action and the explicit ghost operator.
Clearly document the choice of gauge-fixing conditions.
Demonstrate that the resulting ghost fields correctly cancel any unphysical degrees of freedom.
Include a derivation of the transformation properties under both diffeomorphism and the extra scalar gauge transformation.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Implement gauge fixing and derive the ghost sector

bullet_points: Bring the gauge-fixed quadratic operator and ghost operator into standard heat kernel form.
Use the heat kernel (Schwinger-DeWitt) method to compute the Seeley-DeWitt $a_2$ coefficients.
Extract the UV-divergent part of the one-loop effective action.
Output the explicit counter-term Lagrangian $\mathcal{L}_{ct}$ as a function of the background fields and their derivatives.
Ensure the correct normalization conventions when reshaping the operators into standard heat kernel form.
Double-check that the Seeley-DeWitt coefficients are computed consistently and address any differences in operator ordering or potential terms explicitly.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Compute the one-loop effective action and extract UV divergences

bullet_points: Match the counter-term Lagrangian to the operator structure of the original action.
Derive the beta-functionals for $F$, $A_1$, $A_3$, $A_4$, and $A_5$.
Compute the RG-time derivatives of the degeneracy constraints using the chain rule.
Output the explicit expressions for the beta-functionals and the RG flow of the degeneracy constraints.
Clearly match each term of the counter-term Lagrangian with the original action operator structure.
Provide detailed intermediate derivations for the beta-functionals.
Include cross-checks using symbolic manipulation to ensure robustness of the chain-rule calculations applied to the degeneracy constraints.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Extract beta-functionals for the defining functions and degeneracy constraints

bullet_points: Assess whether the RG flow is tangent to the degeneracy manifold by evaluating the RG-time derivatives of the constraints.
Discuss the implications for quantum stability or instability of the Type Ia class.
Interpret the physical meaning of the results, including the fate of the Ostrogradsky ghost at one-loop.
Summarize the main findings and their significance for the quantum consistency of higher-order scalar-tensor gravity.
Prepare a thorough Results section that interprets the RG flow with respect to the degeneracy manifold and discusses potential physical implications, including the fate of the Ostrogradsky ghost.

Summarize the key quantitative findings and include precise references to the plots or computed expressions generated by the engineer, ensuring all necessary details for the paper writer agents are present.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Analyze and interpret the quantum stability of the degeneracy manifold
